claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and the children you're asking about.

Let me start with a few questions:

**First, how many children are you asking about, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking into whether I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 16 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have one child, Alex, who is 16 years old and lives with you in the UK.

I need to ask a few more questions to assess eligibility properly.

**Is Alex in full-time education or training, or is he not currently in education/training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's actually just left school recently. He's registered with the careers service to figure out what he wants to do next - you know, getting advice on his options for college or work.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. It's good that Alex is getting support from the careers service.

**Can you tell me - is Alex currently in any full-time education or approved training programme, or is he in a period where he's not yet started anything?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training programme at the moment. He just finished school and signed up with the careers service, but hasn't started anything yet.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. I have one more important question before I can give you my assessment.

**What is your residency status in the UK? Are you a British citizen, or do you have a different immigration status (such as settled status, visa, etc.)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a British citizen, yes. I live here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Before I make my final assessment, let me just check one more detail to be thorough.

**Are you currently receiving any benefits such as Universal Credit, Tax Credits, or other state support?** (This helps me understand the full picture, though it doesn't necessarily affect eligibility.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not receiving any benefits at the moment. I'm just trying to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility. Let me review the Child Benefit rules carefully by checking the official guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the official Child Benefit eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can now provide you with my assessment.

**Good news!** You should be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex for a limited period.

Here's why: According to the rules, while children normally need to be under 16 to qualify, there's an important exception. For children aged 16 or over who have **left education or training**, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for **20 weeks** if they register with a government-sponsored careers service.

Since Alex has just left school and is registered with the careers service, he qualifies for this 20-week extension period. After this 20-week period ends, eligibility will depend on whether Alex has entered approved education or training by that point.

You meet all the other requirements:
- You're responsible for Alex (he lives with you)
- You're a British citizen living in the UK
- You're the only person claiming for him

**Important note:** This 20-week period is time-limited, so it's worth keeping track of when it started and planning ahead for what happens after those 20 weeks are up.

Let me now formally record this assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and has recently left school. He is registered with a government-sponsored careers service. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, when a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with a government-sponsored careers service, the claimant can continue to receive Child Benefit for 20 weeks. The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK and is responsible for Alex (he lives with the claimant). Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex for a 20-week period from when he registered with the careers service.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their son Alex. Alex qualifies under the special 20-week extension rule for children aged 16-17 who have left education or training and registered with a government-sponsored careers service. The claimant meets all other eligibility criteria as a British citizen living in the UK. Eligibility will cease after 20 weeks unless Alex enters approved education or training during that period.">
